betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Seal Detector§f is a §6single block§f utility tile that detects whether a §6sealed room§f is truly airtight — it lights up red when the room has any §6unsealed edges§f that would leak oxygen to vacuum. It is the §6diagnostic tool§f for every AR habitat build, used to validate airlocks, oxygen vent coverage, and §6Space Suit§f pressure seals.

§6Production Path (sealdetector.json)§f:
- Type: §6minecraft:crafting_shaped§f
- Pattern (3×3):
  ```
  t g t
  . f .
  . c .
  ```
- Key: `t`=§6minecraft:redstone_torch§f, `g`=§6paneGlass§f (forge:ore_dict), `f`=§6fanSteel§f (forge:ore_dict), `c`=§6minecraft:comparator§f
- Output: 1x §6Seal Detector§f (advancedrocketry:sealdetector)

The seal detector emits a redstone signal proportional to the §6leakage rate§f of the room — a fully sealed room gives a §60§f signal, a heavily leaking room gives a §615§f signal. Place the detector inside the room to be tested; it will pulse redstone through the walls if any edge is unsealed.

The seal detector is a prerequisite for the §6Oxygen Vent§f (10051) and the §6Airlock Door§f (10053). It is also used by the §6Space Suit§f (10049) GUI to display the §6suit pressure§f status — green for sealed, red for compromised.

§eTip§f: The detector's redstone output can be wired to a §6NOT gate§f and an §6alarm§f for automated leak detection. For complex builds, place one detector per §620×20§f room — multiple detectors do not interfere with each other.
